title: MCQ – Chapters 13 to 17 Mixed (200 Questions)
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav (6th Edition) – Chapters 13–17
section: Muscle Relaxants, Perioperative Complications, Local Anesthetics, Peripheral Nerve Blocks, Central Neuraxial Blocks

questions.question: Which muscle relaxant is the ideal choice for intubation due to early onset and short duration?
questions.explanation: Because of early onset and short duration Succinylcholine is the ideal muscle relaxant for intubation.

questions.question: The most common cause of anesthesia-related death in perioperative period is:
questions.explanation: Postoperative respiratory depression has been cited as the most common cause of anesthesia related death.

questions.question: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine without adrenaline is:
questions.explanation: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine without adrenaline is 4.5 mg/kg (maximum 300 mg).

questions.question: The most bothersome complication of supraclavicular block is:
questions.explanation: The most bothersome complication of supraclavicular block is pneumothorax.

questions.question: Hyperbaric bupivacaine is the most commonly used drug for:
questions.explanation: Hyperbaric bupivacaine is the most commonly used drug for spinal.

questions.question: Hyperkalemia is the most prominent effect of:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia is the most prominent effect of suxamethonium.

questions.question: More than 80% of cardiac arrest during anesthesia occurs at the time of:
questions.explanation: More than 80% of cardiac arrest occurs at the time of induction.

questions.question: Chloroprocaine is the shortest acting local anesthetic while the longest acting is:
questions.explanation: Chloroprocaine is the shortest acting and dibucaine is the longest acting local anesthetic.

questions.question: Ulnar nerve is usually spared with which approach of brachial plexus block?
questions.explanation: Ulnar nerve is usually spared with interscalene approach.

questions.question: The most common complication of spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Hypotension is the most common complication of spinal anesthesia.

questions.question: Vecuronium is the most cardiovascular stable:
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is most cardiovascular stable muscle relaxant.

questions.question: Full stomach is the single most important predisposing factor for:
questions.explanation: Full stomach is the single most important predisposing factor for aspiration.

questions.question: Central nervous system is the first system involved in:
questions.explanation: Central nervous system is the first system involved in local anesthetic toxicity.

questions.question: Cervical plexus block is best utilized for:
questions.explanation: Cervical plexus block is best utilized for carotid endarterectomy.

questions.question: The sequence of blockade after central neuraxial block is:
questions.explanation: The sequence of blockade after CNB is autonomic > sensory > motor.

questions.question: Rocuronium is the nondepolarizer of choice for:
questions.explanation: Rocuronium is nondepolarizer of choice for intubation.

questions.question: The most common postoperative pulmonary complication is:
questions.explanation: Atelectasis is the most common postoperative pulmonary complication.

questions.question: Allergic reactions are common with which type of local anesthetics?
questions.explanation: Allergic reactions are common with esters but rare with amides.

questions.question: Maximum systemic absorption of local anesthetic occurs after:
questions.explanation: Maximum systemic absorption of local anesthetic occurs after intercostal nerve block.

questions.question: Ephedrine is the most preferred vasopressor for treatment of:
questions.explanation: Ephedrine is the most preferred vasopressor for the treatment of spinal hypotension.

questions.question: Atracurium and cisatracurium are metabolized by Hoffman degradation, making them choice for:
questions.explanation: Atracurium and cisatracurium are metabolized by Hoffman degradation making them relaxant of choice for hepatic and renal failure.

questions.question: Tachycardia is the most common arrhythmia seen in:
questions.explanation: Tachycardia is the most common arrhythmia seen in perioperative period.

questions.question: Bupivacaine is absolutely contraindicated for:
questions.explanation: Because of high cardiotoxicity bupivacaine is absolutely contraindicated for Bier's block.

questions.question: Only safe local anesthetics permitted for Bier block are lignocaine and:
questions.explanation: Only safe local anesthetics, i.e. lignocaine and prilocaine are permitted for Bier block.

questions.question: The drug of choice for treatment of shivering after spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: The drug of choice for treatment of shivering is pethidine.

questions.question: Mivacurium is the muscle relaxant of choice for:
questions.explanation: Mivacurium is the muscle relaxant of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Secretions is the most common cause of:
questions.explanation: Secretions is the most common cause of laryngospasm in anesthesia.

questions.question: Methemoglobinemia is usually seen with:
questions.explanation: Methemoglobinemia is usually seen with prilocaine.

questions.question: Associated coagulopathy and infection at needle site are absolute contraindications for:
questions.explanation: Associated coagulopathy and infection at needle site are the absolute contraindication for nerve blocks.

questions.question: Urinary retention is the most common postoperative complication of:
questions.explanation: Urinary retention is the most common postoperative complication of central neuraxial blocks in present day practice.

questions.question: Sugammadex rapidly reverses the block by:
questions.explanation: Sugammadex rapidly and completely reverses the block by steroidal type of nondepolarizers but cannot reverse benzylisoquinoline compounds.

questions.question: The most common cause of hypotension in perioperative period is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of hypotension in perioperative period is intravascular volume depletion.

questions.question: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine WITH adrenaline is:
questions.explanation: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine with adrenaline is 7 mg/kg (maximum 500 mg).

questions.question: The use of ultrasound has revolutionized the practice of:
questions.explanation: The use of ultrasound has not only made the nerve blocks technically easy but also has improved the precision.

questions.question: The line joining highest point on iliac crest corresponds to:
questions.explanation: The line joining the highest point on iliac crest corresponds to L4 and L5 interspace or L4 spine.

questions.question: Gallamine is the only nondepolarizer absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy because it:
questions.explanation: Gallamine is the only nondepolarizer which can cross the placenta therefore absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy.

questions.question: Pain is the most common complication in:
questions.explanation: Pain is the most common complication in postoperative period.

questions.question: Amiodarone is the drug of choice for treatment of bupivacaine-induced:
questions.explanation: Amiodarone is considered as drug of choice for the treatment of bupivacaine induced ventricular tachycardia.

questions.question: Musculocutaneous nerve is usually spared with which approach?
questions.explanation: Musculocutaneous is usually spared with axillary approach.

questions.question: Spinal cord extends from medulla oblongata to lower border of L1 in adults and which level in infants?
questions.explanation: Spinal cord extends from medulla oblongata to lower border of L1 in adults and L3 in infants.

questions.question: Fading after suxamethonium is pathognomonic of:
questions.explanation: Fading after suxamethonium is pathognomonic of phase II block.

questions.question: Muscle relaxants are the most common cause of anaphylaxis followed by:
questions.explanation: Muscle relaxants are the most common cause of anaphylaxis followed by latex allergy.

questions.question: Local anesthetics with adrenaline can cause necrosis if used for ring block because these structures have:
questions.explanation: Local anesthetics with adrenaline can cause necrosis and gangrene if used for ring block of fingers, toes, penis or pinna because these structures have end arteries.

questions.question: Keeping the direction of needle lateral to subclavian artery is the best way to avoid:
questions.explanation: Keeping the direction of needle lateral to the Subclavian artery is the best way to avoid pneumothorax.

questions.question: The most common cause of nausea and vomiting after spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of nausea and vomiting is hypotension.

questions.question: Onset of a nondepolarizer is inversely proportional to:
questions.explanation: Onset of a nondepolarizer is inversely proportional to potency.

questions.question: Nitroglycerin as continuous infusion is the most commonly used agent to produce:
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerin as continuous infusion is the most commonly used agent to produce controlled hypotension.

questions.question: Prilocaine has significant:
questions.explanation: Prilocaine has significant extrahepatic metabolism.

questions.question: Brachial plexus block is the second most commonly performed block after:
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus block is the second most commonly performed block (after central neuraxial block).

questions.question: Dose and baricity are the 2 very important determinants of:
questions.explanation: Dose and Baricity are 2 very important determinants of level of block.

questions.question: Succinylcholine is the most commonly implicated drug for:
questions.explanation: Succinylcholine is the most commonly implicated drug for malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Delayed recovery is the most common CNS complication in:
questions.explanation: Delayed recovery is the most common CNS complication.

questions.question: Potency of local anesthetics is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Potency is proportional to the duration of action.

questions.question: Phrenic nerve block occurs in almost all patients with which approach?
questions.explanation: Phrenic nerve block occurs in almost all patients with interscalene approach.

questions.question: Use of small gauge and dura separating needles are most important preventive measures for:
questions.explanation: Use of small gauge and dura separating needles are the most important preventive measures to prevent post spinal headache.

questions.question: Benzylisoquinoline compounds release histamine while steroidal compounds are:
questions.explanation: Benzylisoquinoline releases histamine while steroidal compounds are vagolytic.

questions.question: Cautery is responsible for what percentage of fires in OT?
questions.explanation: Cautery is responsible for 68% of fires seen in OT.

questions.question: Cardiotoxicity of ropivacaine is far lesser than:
questions.explanation: Cardiotoxicity and CNS toxicity of ropivacaine is far lesser than bupivacaine.

questions.question: Interscalene block is indicated for surgery on:
questions.explanation: The usual indication for interscalene block is surgery on the shoulder or upper arm.

questions.question: 6th is the most commonly involved cranial nerve after:
questions.explanation: 6th is most commonly involved cranial nerve after spinal anesthesia.

questions.question: Treatment of choice for low and atypical pseudocholinesterase is:

options.C: Continue IPPV and wait for spontaneous recovery

questions.explanation: Continue intermittent positive pressure ventilation and wait for spontaneous recovery is the treatment of choice.

questions.question: Brachial plexus injuries are the most common postoperative nerve injury associated with:
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus injuries are the most common postoperative nerve injury associated with general anesthesia.

questions.question: Wide differential blockade of bupivacaine and ropivacaine makes them choice for:

options.B: Postoperative pain relief and painless labor

questions.explanation: Wide differential blockade of bupivacaine and ropivacaine makes them local anesthetic of choice for postoperative pain relief and painless labor.

questions.question: Supraclavicular approach blocks which part of brachial plexus?
questions.explanation: Distal trunk and proximal division of brachial plexus is blocked by supraclavicular approach.

questions.question: Post spinal headache is a low pressure headache that gets relieved in:
questions.explanation: Postural relation — pain in sitting and standing position and getting relieved in lying down is diagnostic of postspinal headache.

questions.question: Gantacurium onset and duration is almost comparable to:
questions.explanation: Gantacurium onset and duration is almost comparable to suxamethonium.

questions.question: Ondansetron/Granisetron remains the drugs of choice for:
questions.explanation: Ondansetron/Granisetron remains the drugs of choice for treatment as well as prophylaxis for postoperative nausea and vomiting.

questions.question: At clinically used doses, all local anesthetics are vasodilators EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: At clinically used doses all local anesthetics are vasodilators except cocaine, levobupivacaine and ropivacaine.

questions.question: Supraclavicular approach is usually employed for surgeries on:

options.C: Lower arm, elbow, forearm and hand

questions.explanation: Supraclavicular approach is usually employed for surgeries on lower arm, elbow, forearm and hand.

questions.question: The most commonly used needle for epidural is:
questions.explanation: The most commonly used needle for epidural is Tuohy needle.

questions.question: Intraocular, intragastric and intracranial pressures are increased by:
questions.explanation: Intraocular, intragastric and intracranial pressures are increased by suxamethonium.

questions.question: Hyperkalemia-induced ventricular fibrillation is the most common cause of death in:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia induced ventricular fibrillation is the most common cause of death in malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: For spinal and epidural anesthesia, which preparations should be used?
questions.explanation: For spinal and epidural anesthesia preservative free preparations should be used.

questions.question: Axillary approach of brachial plexus blocks at the level of:
questions.explanation: Axillary approach blocks at the level of terminal branches.

questions.question: Streptococcus viridans is most common organism responsible for meningitis after:
questions.explanation: Streptococcus viridans has been cited as the most common organism responsible for meningitis after spinal.

questions.question: Cisatracurium does not release histamine and Laudonosine production is how many times less than atracurium?
questions.explanation: Cisatracurium does not release histamine and Laudonosine production is 5 times less than atracurium.

questions.question: Propofol and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants can delay or prevent:
questions.explanation: Propofol and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants can delay or prevent malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Maximum local neurotoxicity has been reported with:
questions.explanation: Maximum local neurotoxicity has been reported with chloroprocaine.

questions.question: Horner syndrome during interscalene block is due to block of:
questions.explanation: Horner syndrome occurs due to block of stellate ganglion during interscalene approach.

questions.question: The most commonly used method to locate epidural space is:
questions.explanation: Most commonly used method to locate epidural space is by loss of resistance technique.

questions.question: In clinical practice, central muscles are blocked and recover earlier than:
questions.explanation: In clinical practice central muscles are blocked and recover earlier than peripheral muscles (limb muscles).

questions.question: The most common cause of atelectasis is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of atelectasis is secretions.

questions.question: Sequence of blockade of nerve fibers by local anesthetics is:
questions.explanation: Sequence of blockade of nerve fibers is A > B > C.

questions.question: Interscalene block — brachial plexus is blocked between anterior and middle scalene at level of:
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus is blocked between anterior and middle scalene at the level of cricoid.

questions.question: It is considered safe to give CNB how many hours after last dose of LMWH?
questions.explanation: It is considered safe to give central neuraxial block 12 hours after last dose of LMWH.

questions.question: Intense bronchospasm led to withdrawal of which muscle relaxant?
questions.explanation: Intense bronchospasm led to withdrawal of rapacuronium from market.

questions.question: Aspiration is considered as a:
questions.explanation: Aspiration is considered as preventable complication of anesthesia.

questions.question: Local anesthetics are classified on the basis of chemical structure and:
questions.explanation: Local anesthetics are classified on the basis of chemical structure and duration of action.

questions.question: For Bier's block, drug with high toxicity like bupivacaine is:
questions.explanation: Drug with high toxicity like bupivacaine is absolutely contraindicated for Bier block.

questions.question: Fentanyl + bupivacaine or ropivacaine as continuous infusion is most commonly used for:
questions.explanation: Fentanyl + bupivacaine or ropivacaine as continuous infusion is the most commonly used combination for postoperative analgesia and painless labor.

questions.question: To prevent muscarinic side effects, anticholinergic has to be given with:
questions.explanation: To prevent muscarinic side effects, anticholinergic like atropine or glycopyrrolate has to be given with cholinesterase inhibitors.

questions.question: Rapid sequence induction must be employed for patients at high risk of:
questions.explanation: Rapid sequence induction technique must be employed for giving GA to patients at high risk of aspiration.

questions.question: Recent studies prove local anesthetics block sodium channels AND also block:
questions.explanation: Local anesthetics not only act on sodium channels, but also block potassium and calcium channels.

questions.question: Pneumothorax incidence with supraclavicular block is:
questions.explanation: Pneumothorax incidence is 1–6% with supraclavicular block.

questions.question: Patients on aspirin and NSAIDs can be safely given:
questions.explanation: Patients on aspirin and NSAIDs can be safely given central neuraxial blocks.

questions.question: Sustained jaw clench over a tongue blade is considered as best clinical sign for:
questions.explanation: Sustained jaw clench over a tongue blade is considered as best clinical sign for recovery.

questions.question: Use of oxygen analyzers is mandatory as per:
questions.explanation: Use of oxygen analyzers is mandatory as per ASA task force guidelines.

questions.question: Mepivacaine, bupivacaine, levobupivacaine and ropivacaine cannot be used for:
questions.explanation: Mepivacaine, bupivacaine, levobupivacaine and ropivacaine cannot be used for surface anesthesia.

questions.question: Infraclavicular approach blocks brachial plexus at the level of:
questions.explanation: Infraclavicular approach blocks at the level of cords.

questions.question: Transient neurological symptoms and cauda equina syndrome led to almost elimination of which drug for spinal?
questions.explanation: Transient neurological symptoms and cauda equina syndrome lead to almost elimination of lignocaine for spinal.

questions.question: Succinylcholine increases all pressures EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Intraocular, intragastric and intracranial pressures are increased by suxamethonium (not specifically blood pressure).

questions.question: Maximum complications during anesthesia are reported in which period?
questions.explanation: Maximum complications are reported in maintenance period.

questions.question: The shortest acting local anesthetic is:
questions.explanation: Chloroprocaine is the shortest acting local anesthetic.

questions.question: Unilateral phrenic nerve block has clinically significant consequences reducing pulmonary functions by:
questions.explanation: Unilateral phrenic nerve block reduces pulmonary functions by only 20–25%.

questions.question: Patients with platelet count more than how much can safely receive CNB?
questions.explanation: Patients with platelet count more than 80,000/cubic mm can be safely given central neuraxial blocks.

questions.question: Rocuronium is a nondepolarizer of which chemical type?
questions.explanation: Rocuronium is a steroidal nondepolarizer (therefore can be reversed by sugammadex).

questions.question: Halothane is the most commonly implicated inhalational agent for causing:
questions.explanation: Halothane is the most commonly implicated inhalational agent for causing malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Local anesthetics for ring block of fingers, toes, penis or pinna should NOT contain:
questions.explanation: Local anesthetics with adrenaline can cause necrosis and gangrene if used for ring block because these structures have end arteries.

questions.question: Axillary approach is NOT suitable for:
questions.explanation: Axillary approach spares musculocutaneous nerve making it unsuitable for shoulder surgery.

questions.question: CNB should be delayed for 7 days after:
questions.explanation: CNB should be delayed for 7 days after clopidogrel.

questions.question: Vecuronium is the muscle relaxant of choice for:
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is the muscle relaxant of choice for cardiac patients due to cardiovascular stability.

questions.question: Postoperative hypertension and tachycardia carry higher morbidity than:
questions.explanation: Postoperative hypertension and tachycardia are more responsible for unplanned ICU admission and carry higher morbidity and mortality than hypotension and bradycardia.

questions.question: Maximum systemic absorption has been seen after:
questions.explanation: Maximum systemic absorption has been seen after intercostal nerve block.

questions.question: Needle direction for supraclavicular block should be lateral to:
questions.explanation: Needle is inserted lateral to subclavian vessels.

questions.question: LMWH can be safely given how many hours after central neuraxial blocks?
questions.explanation: LMWH can be given safely 6 hours after central neuraxial blocks.

questions.question: Anticholinergic preferred with cholinesterase inhibitors includes atropine or:
questions.explanation: Anticholinergic like atropine or glycopyrrolate has to be given with cholinesterase inhibitors.

questions.question: Anesthetic drugs which can cause convulsion include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Anesthetic drugs which can cause convulsion are—Local anesthetics, methohexitone, enflurane, sevoflurane and atracurium/cisatracurium (not propofol).

questions.question: Lignocaine and prilocaine are the ONLY safe local anesthetics for:
questions.explanation: Only safe local anesthetics, i.e. lignocaine and prilocaine are permitted for Bier block.

questions.question: Epidural and intrathecal injection is a dreadful complication of which approach?
questions.explanation: Epidural and intrathecal injection can be dreadful complication of interscalene approach.

questions.question: High spinal is associated with:
questions.explanation: High spinal is associated with bradycardia and hypotension.

questions.question: Mivacurium is metabolized by:
questions.explanation: Mivacurium is metabolized by plasma cholinesterase making it short-acting for day care surgery.

questions.question: Death is the leading cause of claims as per:
questions.explanation: Death is the leading cause of claims as per American Society of Anesthesiologists (ASA) claim studies.

questions.question: The traditional notion that thin fibers are most sensitive to local anesthetics:

options.B: Does not hold true in present day practice

questions.explanation: The traditional notion that thin fibers are most sensitive to the action of local anesthetics does not hold true in present day practice.

questions.question: For interscalene block, needle is inserted between:
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus is blocked between anterior and middle scalene.

questions.question: There is less hypotension in epidural as compared to:
questions.explanation: There is less hypotension in epidural as compared to spinal.

questions.question: Train of four ratio > 0.9 guarantees recovery. A ratio of 0.7 indicates:
questions.explanation: The ratio of 0.7 indicates adequate recovery but recovery is guaranteed only at a ratio of >0.9.

questions.question: The most common complication in postoperative period is:
questions.explanation: Pain is the most common complication in postoperative period.

questions.question: Cocaine is unique among local anesthetics because it causes:
questions.explanation: At clinically used doses all local anesthetics are vasodilators except cocaine, levobupivacaine and ropivacaine.

questions.question: Needle insertion point for supraclavicular block is 1 cm superior to:
questions.explanation: Needle is inserted at a point 1 cm superior to midpoint of clavicle.

questions.question: Staphylococcus epidermidis is the most common organism responsible for meningitis after:
questions.explanation: Staphylococcus epidermidis is the most common organism responsible for meningitis after epidural.

questions.question: Suxamethonium is the ideal relaxant for intubation but restricted to rapid sequence or difficult intubation due to:
questions.explanation: Because of the side effects its use is only restricted to rapid sequence or difficult intubation.

questions.question: Hypothermia is the most common thermal perturbation seen in anesthesia. It increases wound infection by:
questions.explanation: Hypothermia increases the chances of wound infection by 3 times.

questions.question: Ropivacaine cardiotoxicity is lesser than bupivacaine and lesser than levobupivacaine but higher than:
questions.explanation: Cardiotoxicity of ropivacaine is still higher than lignocaine.

questions.question: Horner syndrome during brachial plexus block is due to block of stellate ganglion which causes:
questions.explanation: Horner syndrome (ptosis, miosis, anhidrosis, enophthalmos) occurs due to block of stellate ganglion.

questions.question: Chances of infectious complications, epidural hematoma and neurotoxicity is more with:
questions.explanation: Chances of infectious complications, epidural hematoma and neurotoxicity is more with epidural as compared to spinal.

questions.question: Phase II block is seen in overdosage of succinylcholine or abnormal:
questions.explanation: Phase II block is seen in over dosage of succinylcholine or abnormal plasma cholinesterase activity.

questions.question: After regional anesthesia, the most commonly involved nerves are:
questions.explanation: After regional anesthesia lumbosacral nerve roots are most commonly involved nerves.

questions.question: For postoperative pain and painless labor, the local anesthetics of choice are bupivacaine and:
questions.explanation: Wide differential blockade of bupivacaine and ropivacaine makes them local anesthetic of choice for postoperative pain relief and painless labor.

questions.question: Needle insertion for supraclavicular block: needle is inserted in which direction?
questions.explanation: The needle is inserted in downward and backward direction till paresthesia is elicited.

questions.question: Apnea after spinal anesthesia is usually due to:
questions.explanation: Apnea after spinal anesthesia is usually due to severe hypotension.

questions.question: Central muscles blocked first include:
questions.explanation: In clinical practice central muscles (diaphragm, larynx) are blocked and recover earlier than peripheral muscles.

questions.question: Anesthetic drugs that can cause convulsion include local anesthetics, methohexitone, enflurane, sevoflurane and:
questions.explanation: Anesthetic drugs which can cause convulsion include atracurium/cisatracurium (due to laudanosine).

questions.question: The longest acting local anesthetic is:
questions.explanation: Dibucaine is the longest acting local anesthetic.

questions.question: Volume of local anesthetic for supraclavicular block is:
questions.explanation: 20–30 mL of 1–1.5% xylocaine alone or mixed with bupivacaine is injected.

questions.question: Several meta-analyses show CNB reduces overall mortality and morbidity by:
questions.explanation: Several meta-analyses have shown a reduced risk as high as 30% in overall mortality and morbidity in patients who underwent surgeries under CNB.

questions.question: Rocuronium is the nondepolarizer of choice for intubation because of its:
questions.explanation: Rocuronium has rapid onset making it the nondepolarizer of choice for intubation.

questions.question: The most common thermal perturbation seen in anesthesia that triples morbid cardiac outcomes is:
questions.explanation: Hypothermia triples the incidence of morbid cardiac outcomes.

questions.question: Methemoglobinemia can also be caused by:
questions.explanation: Methemoglobinemia is usually seen with prilocaine however benzocaine can also cause methemoglobinemia.

questions.question: For lower limb surgeries using brachial plexus, which is NOT an approach?
questions.explanation: Femoral nerve block is for lower limb, not brachial plexus.

questions.question: Postspinal headache is:
questions.explanation: Postspinal headache is low pressure, meningovascular headache.

questions.question: Sugammadex CANNOT reverse:
questions.explanation: Sugammadex cannot reverse benzylisoquinoline compounds (like atracurium).

questions.question: The most common postoperative nerve injury after GA is:
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus injuries are the most common postoperative nerve injury associated with general anesthesia.

questions.question: Allergic reactions with local anesthetics are common with esters but rare with:
questions.explanation: Allergic reactions are common with esters but rare with amides.

questions.question: Axillary approach spares which nerve making it unsuitable for hand surgery at ulnar border?
questions.explanation: Musculocutaneous nerve is usually spared with axillary approach.

questions.question: The most preferred vasopressor for spinal hypotension is:
questions.explanation: Ephedrine is the most preferred vasopressor for the treatment of spinal hypotension.

questions.question: Atracurium is metabolized by Hoffman degradation — this makes it ideal for patients with:
questions.explanation: Atracurium is metabolized by Hoffman degradation making it relaxant of choice for hepatic and renal failure.

questions.question: Rapid sequence induction includes:

options.A: Slow induction with careful mask ventilation
options.B: Preoxygenation, rapid IV induction, cricoid pressure, intubation without mask ventilation

questions.explanation: Rapid sequence induction technique must be employed for patients at high risk of aspiration.

questions.question: Which local anesthetic has significant extrahepatic metabolism making it safer for liver disease?
questions.explanation: Prilocaine has significant extrahepatic metabolism.

questions.question: Axillary approach: needle is inserted after palpating which artery?
questions.explanation: For axillary approach needle is inserted adjacent to axillary artery.

questions.question: CNB reduces overall mortality by 30% compared to:
questions.explanation: Several meta-analyses have shown a reduced risk as high as 30% in overall mortality in patients under CNB compared to GA.

questions.question: The best clinical sign for adequate recovery from neuromuscular block is:

options.C: Sustained jaw clench over tongue blade

questions.explanation: Sustained jaw clench over a tongue blade is considered as best clinical sign for recovery.

questions.question: Tachycardia is the most common arrhythmia but the most common cause of death in MH is:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia induced ventricular fibrillation is the most common cause of death in malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Drug of choice for bupivacaine-induced VT is now:
questions.explanation: Amiodarone is considered as drug of choice for bupivacaine induced ventricular tachycardia.

questions.question: In Bier's block the tourniquet pressure should be above systolic by:
questions.explanation: Tourniquet pressure should be 100 mmHg above systolic for Bier's block.

questions.question: Hyperbaric solution is heavier than CSF so patient position determines:
questions.explanation: Baricity (and dose) are 2 very important determinants of level of block. Position determines spread of hyperbaric solution.

questions.question: Which nondepolarizer has onset and duration almost comparable to succinylcholine?
questions.explanation: Gantacurium onset and duration is almost comparable to suxamethonium.

questions.question: The most common cause of laryngospasm during anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Secretions is the most common cause of laryngospasm in anesthesia.

questions.question: For epidural and spinal anesthesia, preparations should be:
questions.explanation: For spinal and epidural anesthesia preservative free preparations should be used.

questions.question: Ultrasound guidance for nerve blocks has:

options.B: Made them technically easy and improved precision

questions.explanation: The use of ultrasound has not only made the nerve blocks technically easy but also has improved the precision.

questions.question: Epidural space is located at which distance from skin in average adult?
questions.explanation: Epidural space is usually located 4-6 cm from skin in adults.

questions.question: Gallamine crosses the placenta and is therefore contraindicated in:
questions.explanation: Gallamine is the only nondepolarizer which can cross the placenta therefore absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy.

questions.question: The leading cause of claims in anesthesia practice as per ASA studies is:
questions.explanation: Death is the leading cause of claims as per ASA claim studies.

questions.question: LA toxicity: which system is first affected?
questions.explanation: Central nervous system is the first system involved in local anesthetic toxicity.

questions.question: Coagulopathy is an absolute contraindication for:
questions.explanation: Associated coagulopathy is the absolute contraindication for nerve blocks.

questions.question: Most common cause of nausea/vomiting after spinal is hypotension. Most common postop complication of CNB in present day is:
questions.explanation: Urinary retention is the most common postoperative complication of central neuraxial blocks in present day practice.

questions.question: Laudanosine is a metabolite of atracurium that can cause:
questions.explanation: Laudanosine (metabolite of atracurium) can cause CNS excitation. Cisatracurium produces 5 times less.

questions.question: Aspiration prophylaxis in high-risk patients includes metoclopramide, antacids and:
questions.explanation: Drugs for aspiration prophylaxis: metoclopramide, antacids and H2 blockers (ranitidine).

questions.question: Local anesthetics block nerve fibers in order A > B > C. 'A' fibers are:
questions.explanation: A fibers are large myelinated (motor and sensory) blocked first.

questions.question: For carotid endarterectomy, the block of choice is:
questions.explanation: Cervical plexus block is best utilized for carotid endarterectomy.

questions.question: The most commonly used drug for spinal in India is:
questions.explanation: Hyperbaric bupivacaine is the most commonly used drug for spinal.

questions.question: Depolarizing block (Phase I) shows which pattern on train of four?

options.B: Equal decrease in all four responses

questions.explanation: With depolarizing muscle relaxants all four responses decrease simultaneously in amplitude, i.e. T4/T1 remains 1.

questions.question: The most common arrhythmia in perioperative period is tachycardia. Most common cause of perioperative hypotension is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of hypotension in perioperative period is intravascular volume depletion.

questions.question: Ropivacaine has less cardiotoxicity and CNS toxicity compared to bupivacaine. This is because ropivacaine is:
questions.explanation: Ropivacaine is the S-enantiomer with less lipid solubility hence less toxic.

questions.question: Interscalene approach blocks at the level of:
questions.explanation: Blockade occurs at the level of the superior and middle trunks with interscalene approach.

questions.question: Epidural analgesia technique of choice for painless labor uses:
questions.explanation: Continuous lumbar epidural is the most commonly used technique for painless labor.

questions.question: Histamine release is a characteristic of which class of nondepolarizers?
questions.explanation: Benzylisoquinoline releases histamine while steroidal compounds are vagolytic.

questions.question: The most common cause of anaphylaxis in OT is muscle relaxants. Second most common is:
questions.explanation: Muscle relaxants are the most common cause of anaphylaxis followed by latex allergy.

questions.question: Intralipid (lipid emulsion) therapy is used for rescue in severe:
questions.explanation: Intralipid therapy is the rescue treatment for severe local anesthetic systemic toxicity (LAST).

questions.question: 20-30 mL of local anesthetic is used for:
questions.explanation: 20–30 mL of local anesthetic is injected for supraclavicular block.

questions.question: Pencil-point (dura separating) needles for spinal include Whitacre and:
questions.explanation: Dura separating (pencil-point) needles include Whitacre and Sprotte.

questions.question: Sugammadex works by:
questions.explanation: Sugammadex rapidly reverses block by encapsulating steroidal nondepolarizer molecules.

questions.question: Controlled hypotension is produced most commonly by:
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerin as continuous infusion is the most commonly used agent to produce controlled hypotension.

questions.question: Bupivacaine is the local anesthetic most associated with:
questions.explanation: Because of high cardiotoxicity bupivacaine is absolutely contraindicated for Bier's block.

questions.question: Which nerve block provides analgesia for surgeries below the clavicle on upper limb?
questions.explanation: Infraclavicular approach is suitable for surgeries below the clavicle on upper limb.

questions.question: Epidural space is identified by Tuohy needle using loss of resistance to:
questions.explanation: Most commonly used method to locate epidural space is by loss of resistance technique (using air or saline).

questions.question: Neuromuscular block adequate for most surgeries is achieved at TOF count of:
questions.explanation: Absence of T4 response means 75% blockade which is sufficient for most surgeries.

questions.question: Perioperative fires in OT — cautery accounts for 68%. The fuel is usually:
questions.explanation: Cautery is the ignition source, oxygen/N2O supports combustion, and alcohol prep/drapes are fuel.

questions.question: Nerve fibers blocked first (A fibers) recover:
questions.explanation: Larger fibers blocked first recover last (reverse order of blockade).

questions.question: Infection at needle site is absolute contraindication for:
questions.explanation: Infection at needle site is the absolute contraindication for nerve blocks.

questions.question: Central neuraxial block reduces mortality by 30% vs GA mainly by reducing:
questions.explanation: CNB reduces overall mortality by reducing thromboembolism, blood loss and respiratory complications.
